method_atom: a weak-turbulence (wave-kinetic) formalism for an ensemble of weakly coupled waves in which the lowest-order resonant three-wave interactions close into a system of kinetic equations governing the evolution of the spectral densities of the conserved invariants, admitting exact stationary power-law spectra whose prefactor constants depend on the degree of correlation between the two interacting fields.
object_atom: incompressible magnetohydrodynamic turbulence — the coupled velocity and magnetic fields of a conducting fluid, with energy and helicity as the conserved spectral densities, benchmarked against numerical simulations.
brief_reason: The three-wave weak-turbulence closure yielding kinetic equations and correlation-dependent power-law spectra is the transferable machinery, while the coupled velocity/magnetic fields of a conducting fluid are the MHD-specific object.
